title: Rich Umami Vietnamese Pork Bun Bo Hue
description: A flexitarian friendly Vietnamese dish featuring meringue nests and broiled pork, this Bun Bo Hue is a rich and umami indulgent treat. It combines the freshness of herbs with the depth of umami flavors.
instructions: Start by broiling the pork sausage until it is nicely charred.
In a large pot, combine the pork blood, fish sauce, and lemongrass, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
While the broth is simmering, prepare the meringue nests by whipping the egg whites until they become stiff peaks.
Spoon the meringue into nest shapes on a baking sheet and broil until they are golden brown.
Add the shrimp to the simmering broth and cook until they are pink and fully cooked.
Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
Assemble the dish by placing some noodles into a bowl, then adding slices of the broiled pork sausage, some bean sprouts, and a spoonful of the hot broth.
Garnish with a meringue nest, a sprinkle of chili sauce, and a squeeze of lime juice.
